entry.title: Washington Post: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Severodonetsk defenders holding out under merciless shelling, mayor says
  • Dervla Murphy, intrepid author of travel books, dies at 90
  • Kyiv asks for more rocket systems as Kremlin warns of potential strikes in Russia
  • Cholera fears prompt quarantine in Mariupol, official says
  • Is Pope Francis nearing the end of his pontificate?
  • Hopes dim, anger grows in British journalist’s disappearance in Brazil
  • Australians gag as KFC uses cabbage for lettuce in burgers amid shortages
  • What to know about Severodonetsk, the Ukrainian city Russia wants to capture
  • British tourist sentenced to 15 years in Iraqi prison for taking artifacts
  • Sophie Freud, professor who challenged her grandfather’s doctrine, dies at 97
  • Lilibet turns 1, and photos show Harry and Meghan’s daughter loving it
  • China’s university entrance exams are already stressful. Now add covid.
  • The Trump-like TikTok star who could be Colombia’s next president
  • ‘Needle spiking’ fears rise in Europe, but crime ‘really difficult’ to trace
  • Millions of dollars went missing as Afghanistan fell, watchdog says
  • Fighting in Severodonetsk has ‘worsened,’ official says; Britain pledges rocket systems
  • India scrambles to contain fallout over insulting comments about Islam
  • The world’s largest four-day workweek pilot just launched in the U.K.
  • The diplomatic war over Ukrainian grain exports heats up
  • Migrant caravan on the move in southern Mexico
  • Next UN assembly president warns world in dangerous crisis
  • Mexican footbridge collapses during inaugural walk by mayor
  • Police release sketch of girl found in Toronto dumpster
  • Russian cosmonaut who set space endurance record dies
  • No apologies: Germany's Merkel defends approach to Ukraine
  • Neither heroes nor cowards
  • The housing crisis hits mobile homes
  • Teacher wounded in Uvalde shooting says he will never forgive police
  • Trump call Jan. 6 to ‘walk down to the Capitol’ prompted Secret Service scramble
  • Teen killed in ’70s may have been victim of serial killer, detectives say
  • Minn. man impersonating federal agent on TikTok sentenced to six years
  • Mother charged after 2-year-old finds gun, fatally shoots his father
  • Man drowns as Arizona police watch: ‘I’m not jumping in after you’
  • She found $36,000 inside a chair she got for free on Craigslist
  • Primary elections live updates: Mississippi Rep. Steven Palazzo (R) is forced into a runoff
  • McConaughey urges gun measures in surprise White House appearance
  • Jan. 6 hearing will preempt ‘Young Sheldon’ — but not Tucker Carlson
  • Raytheon says it will move its headquarters to Arlington
  • Target cuts prices as pandemic-era inventory piles up
  • World Bank warns global economy may suffer 1970s-style stagflation
  • Crypto industry scores a big win under long-anticipated Senate bill
  • Amazon files motion to close hearing on union victory to the public
  • Meet the YouTubers exposing the dark side of making video games
  • E3 video game convention will return in 2023, says parent company
  • Townhouse community is close to beach but still ‘feels very private’
  • How to bring stamped concrete back to life
  • Contemporary, 300-unit apartment complex opens in Alexandria
  • The Dire Warnings on Inflation-Busting Are Coming True
  • Japan’s Blurred Vision for the Future of Capitalism
  • How Well Is Joe Biden Actually Doing?
  • An Old-Money Billionaire Rises Up Against Adani
  • Meet the Guptas, Symbols of South African Corruption
  • How a Battery Metals Squeeze Puts EV Future at Risk
  • What the Jan. 6 Committee Has Done, and What’s Next
  • What’s Behind Vietnam’s Latest Anti-Corruption Fight
  • The worst nightmare for political prisoners
  • Why Black people are afraid of ‘crazy’ White people
  • Ignore the GOP spin. The nation still hasn’t reckoned with Jan. 6.
  • The U.S.-Russia conflict is heating up — in cyberspace
  • White supremacist attacks stir GOP fears for safety of White people
  • Think things are bad under Biden? Get ready for the blackouts.
  • Trump is gambling big in Wisconsin and Arizona. Losses there could be painful.
  • This Russian journalist blew up her life to protest Putin’s war. Now she’s in limbo.
  • Is there a sickness in U.S. culture? Yes: The GOP gun cult.
  • What inflation struggles of the past can tell the Fed right now
  • The Jan. 6 committee’s audience won’t match Watergate’s. But it should.
  • We already achieved ‘energy independence.’ What good did it do us?
  • Eric Holder on the violent past and imperiled future of voting
  • Myanmar’s military must not hang those fighting for democracy
  • The government is finally tackling ransomware. More work remains.
  • Why is trust in government so low? There’s blame to go around.
  • How Fox News has dominated the White House briefing
  • Matthew McConaughey puts Congress to shame on guns
  • Unreality televison
  • Why did India listen to Arab governments before its own Muslim citizens?
  • The world is finally reacting to India’s descent into hate
  • Goldman lost his Virginia House district suit, but the door is open
  • Wider doors at Thomas Jefferson welcome talented, diverse students
  • An assault weapons ban won’t work
  • The cost of human relationships
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Thai railway market back on track post-pandemic
  • Take-up for electric cars jump, registrations this year already twice the rate for all of 2021: Iswaran
  • Indonesia's FX reserves drop to $135.6 billion at end-May
  • Asia stocks advance as investors weigh impact of hawkish central banks
  • Edmund Chen posts adorable throwback to celebrate 33rd wedding anniversary with Xiang Yun
  • Vietnam considering cutting fuel tax to ease prices - Finance Minister
  • NASA to launch rockets from Australia's north for scientific studies
  • China will adopt targeted measures to help foreign trade firms: vice commerce minister
  • Australia coach wants more from team after World Cup playoff win
  • Blinken says US will 'follow facts' on killing of Al Jazeera journalist Shireen Abu Akleh
  • Saracens flanker Earl named Premiership Player of the Season
  • Former executive alleges Better.com, CEO misled investors to ensure SPAC deal
  • Taiwan's TSMC says no firm plans for now to build Europe factories
  • China to buy 40,000 tonnes of frozen pork for state reserves on June 10 -notice
  • BYD executive says it will supply batteries to Tesla 'very soon'
  • Yen slides as Europe braces for rate hikes
  • Australia and Arnold keep trucking on rocky road to Qatar
  • Japan's Marubeni, J-POWER to fund Glencore's carbon capture project in Australia
  • Australia accuses Airbnb of misleading customers on price
  • Not satisfied with your smile?
  • Germany won't recognise Taliban as 'dire' Afghan conditions persist
  • North Korea could conduct nuclear test 'any time': US envoy
  • Cambodia, China deny naval base reports as Australia voices concern
  • India's ruling party asks officials to exercise caution on religious issues after Islamic nations protest
  • Vietnam's health minister, Hanoi city chief arrested amid anti-graft drive
  • Hong Kong not becoming 'police state', says city's top cop
  • Japan calls for defence spending hike in policy paper, notes threats to Taiwan
  • A week after export ban, chicken prices yet to fall in Malaysian markets
  • China's consumers keep their wallets in lockdown as COVID-19 curbs ease
  • Indonesia, Malaysia join other Muslim countries in condemning 'derogatory' remarks by two India politicians
  • Taiwan appeals to US not to forget its desire for free trade deal
  • 'Gone in front of my eyes': Bangladesh firefighter's unit decimated by blast
  • Myanmar's shadow government to create its own police force
  • Japan to open to tourists after 2 years but only with masks, insurance, guides
  • US, South Korea fly warplanes after North's missile tests
  • Sri Lanka urgently seeks US$6 billion to keep economy afloat
  • China's 'epidemic generation' sits high-stakes college entrance exam
  • Myanmar calls foreign outrage over activists' death sentence 'reckless and interfering'
  • Deadly Bangladesh container depot fire brought under control
  • Relief, caution in Beijing as city lifts COVID-19 dine-in curbs
  • Western Digital reviewing alternatives after Elliott's push to split
  • Singapore trading platform recognises crypto assets of accredited investors
  • Oil prices inch higher ahead of US inventories data
  • Japan's current account surplus shrinks on record imports
  • Japan's Q1 GDP falls less than first thought on stronger consumption
  • South Korea Q1 GDP growth revised slightly down to 0.6%
  • Bolivia still evaluating six firms for lithium mining partnerships

  • Commentary: Why are we all working so hard if it isn't making us richer?
  • Take-up rate for electric cars jump, registrations this year already twice that for whole of 2021: Iswaran
  • Mobile app that identifies sharks’ fins to help curb illegal wildlife trade
  • Promotions, pregnancy and pay: What Singapore women need to know about workplace discrimination
  • Kitchen Stories: Love, death and croissants: The in-demand pastry chef whose sweet tooth conquered all
  • Is petrol costing you a pretty penny? Here are 6 tips on saving money at the pump
  • PUB, SP Group and Singpass impersonated by scammers amid surge in phishing cases: Police
  • HIV-positive man gets jail for not telling sexual partner about risk of infection
  • Cruises from Singapore to Indonesia to resume next month with Genting Dream sailing to Bintan, Batam
  • Jail for company director who obstructed investigations into suspected gasoil supply to North Korea
  • Singapore must specialise and build on strengths to accelerate sustainable solutions: Grace Fu
  • Man jailed for threatening to upload nude videos of ex-girlfriend whom he owed money
  • Why a former real estate agent was fined less than the S$1.25m revenue for subletting apartments on Airbnb, HomeAway
  • Government raises confirmed private housing supply by 26%, to launch first sale site in Marina South
  • Canada to play Curaçao after sitting out prior match over contract dispute
  • Mane scores last-gasp penalty to secure Senegal win
  • Williams fined for 'procedural breach' of F1 cost cap rules
  • Kane penalty earns England draw in Germany
  • Italy back to winning ways with Nations League victory over Hungary
  • Zverev undergoes surgery on torn ligaments in ankle after French Open exit
  • Late deflected strike keeps Australian World Cup hopes alive
  • De Vrij to skipper Netherlands as Van Dijk goes on holiday
  • Australia thrash Sri Lanka by 10 wickets in first T20
  • LIV golfers will still be able to compete in US Open, says USGA
  • Wales captain Bale needs to play regularly before World Cup, says manager
  • Woods to skip US Open, still plans to play British Open
  • Orlando Pride head coach placed on leave amid NWSL investigation
  • No drastic changes for Belgium after heavy Dutch defeat
  • McDowell says LIV Invitational polarising, but proud to be on board
  • Udinese name former player Sottil as their new coach
  • Norway's Hegerberg to play at Women's Euro
  • Novavax COVID-19 shot, aimed at vaccine sceptics, overwhelmingly backed by FDA panel
  • Canada issues monkeypox-related travel notice, warns of potential delays returning home
  • Commentary: Export bans aren't the solution to global food shortages
  • Brazilian police open criminal probe amid search for British journalist
  • Merkel says she doesn't blame herself for not trying hard enough for Ukraine
  • Spain to draw up laws to abolish prostitution
  • Battle in Ukraine's east rages as Zelenskyy vows to retake territory
  • Brazil's Bolsonaro casts doubt on Biden's 2020 election win ahead of meeting him
  • Food shortages are next global health crisis: Expert
  • Bavarian Nordic eyes more monkeypox vaccine orders amid global 'wake-up call'
  • US warns of copycat attacks after Texas school shooting
  • Bruised by sanctions, Russia's Aeroflot plans US$3 billion cash injection
  • UK designates monkeypox as a notifiable disease
  • Ukraine will fight for all territory, stalemate 'not an option', Zelenskyy says
CNA: Breaking News, Singapore News, World and Asia

entry.title: NY Times: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • JR West making scream-detection system to better react to attacks
  • Government to ask public to save electricity this summer, winter
  • Suspect in COVID subsidy fraud fled as scheme began to unravel
  • Japan to open to tourists after two years but only with masks, insurance, guides
  • Universities in Japan continue trend of moving to urban areas
  • Japan, NATO step up ties amid Russia's invasion of Ukraine
  • Opposition attacks ‘Kishida inflation’ ahead of Diet election
  • Honda emerges from isolation, joins partners for electric vehicles
  • Eneos acquires 4,600 electric vehicle chargers from NEC Corp.
  • BOJ governor: Japanese now more tolerant of higher prices

  • U.S., S. Korea fly 20 fighter jets amid North Korea tensions

  • Biden orders emergency steps to boost U.S. solar production
  • Fierce street fighting in Ukraine’s Sievierodonetsk, a pivotal battle for Donbas
  • Damaged, UK’s Boris Johnson scrapes win in party confidence vote
  • 13-year-old go prodigy becomes youngest to tally 100 victories
  • Famed fireworks festival to return to riverside in Akita Prefecture
  • Lisa’s Wanderings Around Japan/ Makino Botanical Garden: Preserving the legacy of Japan’s ‘father of botany’
  • EDITORIAL: Open discourse with public key to reform efforts for Nihon University
  • VOX POPULI: BOJ governor shows he’s out of touch with the ‘mundane world’
  • VOX POPULI: Meteorite soil may show the ‘umami of life’ came from space
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 8 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-08 03:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hongkongers are more overworked and unhappy in their jobs than most of their Asia-Pacific peers, survey finds
  • Ocean zoning can help Hong Kong repair and rejuvenate its precious seas
  • Changes needed to Hong Kong public housing allocation policy to help cut waiting times for most needy, government advisers says
  • Hong Kong proposes banning CBD products within year, after one third of such items sold in city found to contain traces of illegal active ingredient in marijuana
  • Cathay Pacific given access to HK$7.8 billion loan from Hong Kong government for another year
  • Elderly man struck by lightning in Hong Kong as Observatory warns of serious flooding
  • Malaysian professor loses appeal against life sentence for yoga ball murders in Hong Kong
  • Hong Kong remains world’s most expensive city for expats for third straight year, city becoming ‘less attractive’ than regional rivals
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong snack chain Aji Ichiban shuts all 20 retail outlets amid losses from lack of tourists during pandemic
  • Life in Hong Kong’s shoebox housing - complete visual explainer
  • Heavy rain exposes dummy grenade sparking bomb scare at Hong Kong army barracks
  • Hong Kong tram fares to rise to HK$3, a steeper increase than expected, as operator struggles with fall in ridership and shrinking revenue
  • Hong Kong’s self-styled ‘Captain America’ spouted ‘empty words’, legal counsel says in first appeal related to city’s national security law
  • Tickets for Hong Kong Palace Museum to cost between HK$50 and HK$120, with venue to open on July 2
  • Out-of-control truck pins police car against barrier, crashes into 2 vehicles during Hong Kong morning rush hour
  • Nan Hai says creditors are freezing its assets as it comes up short to meet US$350 million of notes due this weekend
  • Hong Kong extends regulatory rules to NFT-based collective investment schemes, warning investors to be mindful of risks
  • Hong Kong leader Carrie Lam defends police action to close off Victoria Park on June 4, saying anniversary of Tiananmen Square crackdown could spur violent activism
  • ESF kindergartens provide the perfect environment for young learners to flourish and grow
  • China offers rewards of over US$15,000 for national security tip-offs
  • Chinese university fires man who said women staff use children to avoid work and should give sex and salaries to male colleagues
  • Chinese academic warns there are legal risks to normalising Covid-19 testing
  • Deep dive for Chinese underwater lab in mission to bottom of South China Sea
  • Taiwan air force jet makes hard landing in US after equipment failure
  • China’s ‘economic momentum’ fuelling June’s GDP growth, skewing quarterly estimates
  • China says all IAEA member states must agree before Aukus nuclear sub project begins
  • China’s Universal Studios to partially reopen after six-week closure due to Covid controls in Beijing
  • China’s regulators exclude Tencent, NetEase as they approve 60 online game titles in June
  • Ant Group emerges as ‘less finance and more tech’ two years after mega IPO was called off, analysts say in wake of ESG report
  • China and US locked in new infowar after Chinese social media claims American manipulation over Xinjiang
  • US coffee giant Starbucks faces more backlash in China over expired products
  • Chinese top influencer Austin Li Jiaqi’s absence imperils live-streaming campaigns of Taobao, various brands for 618 festival
  • Japan and China ‘must think long term to improve ties’ as frictions rise over Taiwan
  • ‘Please don’t forget’: Taiwan appeals to US for free-trade deal
  • Why do giraffes have long necks? It could have been to fight for mates
  • China’s forex reserves climb for the first time in 2022, but yuan depreciation pressure remains
  • China’s falling birth rate prompts Elon Musk to warn of looming ‘population collapse’
  • Lockdowns and Tiananmen amnesia – why Beijing isn’t ready for the Chinese century
  • Can Joe Biden’s economic package overcome Americas summit exclusion row?
  • China’s 30/60 goals: Beijing unveils ‘transition bonds’ for eight dirty industries, to meet carbon reduction targets
  • Gruelling gaokao exams put China’s ‘epidemic generation’ to the test
  • China plans to start building first-ever solar power plant in space by 2028
  • Is monkeypox a ‘side effect’ of AstraZeneca’s Covid-19 vaccine? Korean expert slams ‘very ignorant rumour’
  • Why Indonesians of different faiths are mourning Ahmad Syafi’i Ma’arif, the Islamic scholar who preached ‘co-existence’ over tolerance
  • US must accept Southeast Asia wants China to play a central Indo-Pacific role
  • India’s BJP asks officials to exercise caution on religious issues after Islamic nations protest
  • Indonesian colonel sentenced to life in prison for killing teenagers
  • Japan, Nato strengthen ties amid Russia’s invasion of Ukraine – step up military action
  • Myanmar junta’s ‘cold blooded assassination’: rights groups condemn decision to hang political prisoners
  • Pacific tensions: New Zealand leaders call for less ‘patronising’ approach to island nations
  • Thailand urged to let refugee status of stranded Rohingya be evaluated by UN
  • What caused a Malaysian man to slice his mother into 15 pieces in alleged murder?
  • US, S Korea fly 20 fighter jets over N Korea in ‘swift and forceful’ response, a day after warning
  • Oscar-winning Japanese musician ‘The Last Emperor’ Sakamoto battling stage IV cancer
  • US warns of ‘forceful response’ to anticipated North Korea nuclear test
  • Tourists to Japan may be sent home if they don’t wear face masks and follow other health measures, government says
  • India under fire as Muslim nations lash out over Prophet Mohammed remarks
  • ‘Sauce-obsessed’ US man accused of killing Chinese food delivery worker over sweet-and-sour duck sauce
  • Amid Ukraine war, Angela Merkel defends her Russia legacy, says there’s ‘nothing to apologise for’
  • ‘Unburnable’ copy of Handmaid’s Tale auctioned for US$130,000
  • Brad Pitt says Angelina Jolie sought ‘harm’ by selling vineyard to Russian oligarch
  • Matthew McConaughey makes tearful plea for US gun laws after massacre in Texas hometown
  • Russia claims ‘97 per cent control’ of flashpoint eastern Ukraine Luhansk province
  • Ex-US schoolteacher pleads guilty to leading Islamic State all-woman brigade
  • New ‘doggy bag’ law for bars and restaurants in Spain set to be enforced in order to limit waste
  • French health workers warn of ‘deadly chaos’ in protest over pay, just days before elections
  • Electronic device chargers set to all be the same – no more tangled cables and less waste, EU says
  • Donald Trump reposts edited image of his likeness on Mount Rushmore, next to George Washington
  • Ukraine war: Russia bans 61 more Americans as US media face visa, accreditation denial
  • Blinken says reports of Russia stealing Ukraine grain for profit ‘credible’
  • Ukraine’s Zelensky says Russian troops intend to capture Zaporizhzhia, after fall of Kherson and Mariupol
  • Tencent, Kuaishou lead tech rally in Hong Kong as stock traders bet on end of sector crackdown
  • Mainland Chinese buyers expected to flock to luxury Hong Kong housing now that Shanghai’s lockdown was over, developer says
  • Why US will fail to scuttle China’s economic integration with the rest of Asia
  • Hong Kong proptech start-up Butler expands beyond concierge services, signs up Swire, New World and Sun Hung Kai, eyes Singapore
  • Five key real estate priorities for Asia Pacific companies, and what this means for the post-pandemic office of the future
  • Evergrande loses ally after state-owned China Railway puts its stake in real estate unit up for sale
  • Shanghai reopening: world’s biggest port reaps benefits of higher freight rates as exporters get back on their feet after lockdown
  • Investors brand Greenland’s bond payment extension unfair as credibility of state-backed companies comes into question
  • How 3 insurance professionals have helped Hong Kong tackle challenges such as Covid-19 pandemic
  • Citic Securities tops China’s brokerage industry charts, beating peers in assets, revenue and profit
  • LME in court again, faces US$15.3 million suit from trading firm Jane Street Global for cancelling nickel futures trades
  • Why Shanghai reopening is good for Biden and global economy
  • Hong Kong landlords and sellers should brace themselves for a more realistic property market in the near term
  • Traditional bricks-and-mortar toolmaker turns to digital commerce platform Vtex to help it on its digitalisation journey
  • Shenzhen wants to double semiconductor output by 2025 under new plan to boost tech industry
  • The best seafood by the ocean and street-style dining in Hong Kong, plus Japanese hidden gems: a former fast food promoter’s picks
  • What to see at the newly opened M+ Cinema in Hong Kong’s museum of visual culture
  • K-pop in 2022 so far: Le Sserafim chaos, WJSN’s Queendom 2 win confusion, concert tours missing stars – it’s been an up and down year
  • Gwen Stefani’s make-up line GXVE Beauty, four years in the making, is all her
  • Disney+ K-drama Doctor Lawyer: So Ji-sub returns in punchy medical-legal drama mash-up
  • Why are morel mushrooms so loved by Hong Kong chefs? They’re bursting with savoury flavour and full of amino acids and antioxidants
  • Queer artist Oscar yi Hou blends Asian and American imagery to explore ideas of migration, identity and belonging
  • Lamborghini, Ferrari, Maserati, Ducati and Pagani luxury sports cars and bikes all come from the same part of Italy – a visit to Motor Valley
  • How endangered languages in countries like Indonesia and Malaysia are a vital tool in saving the marine environment
  • Pride Month 2022: how do you talk about LGBTQ issues? Better education would go a long way, says Hong Kong rights advocate
  • Who is Stranger Things 4’s Joseph Quinn? Playing season newbie Eddie Munson, the 29-year-old British actor also appeared in Game of Thrones and hopes to star in Lord of the Rings
  • Move over, Scott Disick! Meet Sofia Richie’s new fiancé, Elliot Grainge: his dad is CEO of Universal Music Group and he founded his own label, 10K Projects – but do Lionel and Nicole approve?
  • The most expensive Kardashian-Jenner properties, ranked: from Kim, Kris, Khloé and Kylie’s Hidden Hills homes, to multimillion-dollar mansions shared with exes Kanye West and Travis Scott
  • Not just for royals: Queen Elizabeth and Meghan Markle don tiaras of course, but now so do Blake Lively, Madonna, Angelababy and other A-listers on the red carpet …
  • K-drama queens who don’t look a day over 25: Jang Na-ra, Jun Ji-hyun, Ha Ji-won, Go Hyun-jung and Han Go-eun share their anti-ageing beauty, fitness and diet tips – here’s how they do it
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 8 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Microplastics found in freshly fallen Antarctic snow for first time
  • The Bedwetter review – Sarah Silverman musical is a crude but kind success
  • ‘A visceral experience of psychosis’: why one artist spent three years painting bipolar disorder
  • After four years, Murugappan family begin journey home to Biloela
  • Praise be: unburnable copy of The Handmaid’s Tale fetches $130,000
  • Cambodia and China deny naval base scheme as Australian PM voices concern
  • New Zealand farmers propose paying for emissions to tackle climate change
  • Pelosi and other top Democrats subpoenaed over Bannon contempt case
  • No regrets over handling of Vladimir Putin, says Angela Merkel
